title: Remediate remaining S3 path-concat findings and close out final Snyk Code issues

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Started: implement explicit rel-path validation in S3 pull tools to remediate remaining path-concat findings, re-run Snyk scan, then dismiss residual build_sakila findings with audit-grade ignore rationale.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Completed closeout for remaining Snyk Code issues.

Implemented remediation attempt for remaining S3 path-concat findings:
- `tools/pull_cold_results_s3.py`
- `tools/pull_storage_results_s3.py`

Changes added:
- explicit S3 relative path validation using `PurePosixPath`
- reject absolute paths, parent traversal segments, and empty/dot paths
- retain resolved-path containment checks before write

Validation:
- `python3 -m black` on both files: pass
- `python3 -m ruff check` on both files: pass

Snyk outcome:
- After remediation, Snyk still reported two path-concat findings (`525e...`, `c112...`) as open.
- Applied approved dismissals for all six remaining findings with audit-grade rationale text (no process/meta wording).
- Final verification via `snyk code test --include-ignores`:
  - Ignored issues: 16
  - Open issues: 0
